_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Protocol 4-4 Pediatric Altered Mental Status and general BLS oral glucose guidance. Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Protocol 4-4: Pediatric Altered Mental Status — Oral Glucose Administration
name: Oral Glucose Administration
indications: Patient is altered (GCS ≤ 14) AND able to swallow safely
Patient is not unresponsive or actively seizing
contraindications: Unable to swallow or absent gag reflex
Unresponsive (GCS ≤ 8) — aspiration risk; defer to ALS for IV dextrose
Confirmed or suspected ingestion where oral route is contraindicated

dosing.dose: One tube (15–25 g glucose gel) per manufacturer instructions
dosing.notes: Repeat if no improvement in 15 minutes per Medical Control

dosing.dose: One tube (15–25 g glucose gel) per manufacturer instructions — NOT weight-based
dosing.notes: Patient cooperation required; do NOT administer if unable to swallow safely

setup: Confirm patient is able to swallow — ask patient to open mouth, look for gag reflex
Position patient upright or sitting — do NOT administer while supine if avoidable
Open glucose gel tube; have suction immediately available
Place gel between inner cheek and lower gum (buccal) if patient cannot swallow actively — allow to dissolve slowly
Alternatively: have patient sip from tube directly if alert enough
Remain at bedside — monitor for vomiting, aspiration, and change in mental status
Reassess GCS and mental status every 5 minutes post-administration
Notify ALS of administration time, dose, and patient response

side_effects: Nausea and vomiting — aspiration risk if LOC decreases; have suction ready
Aspiration if patient loses airway protective reflexes — monitor closely
Transient hyperglycemia — expected; document and report to ALS

six_rights_for_this_drug.verification: Altered mental status AND confirmed or suspected hypoglycemia (BG < 60 mg/dL) — ≥ 3 months old AND able to swallow
Oral glucose gel (e.g., Glutose 15, Insta-Glucose) — confirm label, check expiration date
One tube per manufacturer instructions — not weight-based; do not exceed without Medical Control authorization
Oral (PO) — patient must be able to swallow; place gel between cheek and gum if needed and allow to dissolve
Administer as soon as hypoglycemia confirmed or strongly suspected and patient meets swallowing criteria
Document: blood glucose value (if known), GCS before and after, time administered, patient response, repeat assessments every 5 minutes
